constructs: competitive threat perception physiological arousal behavioral aggression
claim: Marketing-induced scarcity cues activate both psychological threat perception and physiological preparation for aggression, leading to behavioral violence even in resource-rich contexts.
Saturated color captures attention through arousal induction, causing objects with high saturation to appear larger than equivalent low-saturation objects.

constructs: rating distribution self-expression identity signaling polarization
claim: Bimodal rating distributions are perceived as stronger signals of self-expressiveness than unimodal distributions, making polarizing products more desirable for identity-threatened consumers.

constructs: money management stress future financial security well-being income
claim: Current financial stress and future financial security are distinct dimensions of perceived financial well-being with comparable predictive power to health and job satisfaction for overall well-being.

constructs: self-construal group size attention to others preference integration
claim: Interdependent consumers balance self and others' preferences consistently across group sizes, while independent consumers only balance preferences for small groups due to differential attention.

phenomenon: identity clarity protects against change aversion
constructs: self-concept clarity identity signaling subscription retention stability motivation
claim: Consumers with unclear self-concepts retain unused identity-relevant subscriptions to avoid signaling identity change, but avoid acquiring new subscriptions; effects are specific to identity-relevant choices.
